task_completion.description: Verify the motorcycle vehicle type and flow are defined correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal output showing an XML file containing `<vType id="moto_split" vClass="motorcycle" width="0.8" length="2.2" latAlignment="arbitrary" minGapLat="0.1" maxSpeed="20.0"/>` and `<flow id="moto_flow" type="moto_split" begin="0" end="1000" number="150".../>`.

task_completion.description: Verify the SUMO configuration file includes the required lateral resolution and tripinfo output path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal output showing a SUMO configuration file (.sumocfg) containing `<lateral-resolution value="0.8"/>` and `<tripinfo-output value="/home/ga/SUMO_Output/sublane_tripinfos.xml"/>`.

task_completion.description: Verify the SUMO simulation was executed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output showing the execution of the `sumo` command with the configured .sumocfg file, followed by simulation progress or completion logs.

task_completion.description: Verify the tripinfo output file was generated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output (e.g., `ls`, `cat`, or `head`) or a file manager showing the existence and content of `/home/ga/SUMO_Output/sublane_tripinfos.xml`.

task_completion.description: Verify the mode comparison report was generated with the correct format and values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output (e.g., `cat /home/ga/SUMO_Output/mode_comparison.txt`) or a text editor showing the file containing exactly `avg_duration_moto=491.27` and `avg_duration_other=381.56`.

integrity.description: Check that the agent actually ran the SUMO simulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the `sumo` command execution in the terminal history, ensuring the agent didn't just mock the tripinfo output file.

integrity.description: Check that the agent calculated the averages from the tripinfo file rather than hardcoding the expected answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a Python script, bash script, or terminal commands (like `grep`, `awk`, or `xmlstarlet`) used to parse the tripinfo XML and compute the averages. The agent should not just `echo` the final numbers directly into the text file without computation.

privileged_info_for_vlm:
The correct simulation results yield an average duration of 491.27 seconds for 'moto_split' trips and 381.56 seconds for all other trips. The final report should contain exactly:
avg_duration_moto=491.27
avg_duration_other=381.56
